column1: # noha-core behavioral mutation-coverage baseline — the ratchet floor (may only shrink)

# platform-unreachable here, deliberately unobservable everywhere: the whole block is #[cfg(unix)] (a Windows build compiles none of it), and even on unix the dir-fsync is best-effort BY DESIGN — `let _ = d.sync_all()`, the documented durability trade — so no test can observe this filter without simulating power loss. Verified at NOHA_JOBS=1 on 2026-07-19; a contended jobs=8 run falsely "kills" this row (suite flakiness), which is how it briefly vanished from the floor.

# platform-unreachable here, deliberately unobservable everywhere: the CLI twin of the server row above — the same #[cfg(unix)] best-effort dir-fsync in write_atomic. NOTE: the line-agnostic ratchet key (file+op+text) is identical to mnema.rs:125 in lock_store, which is live Windows code and PROVEN killable (five tests fail under its mutation, NOHA_JOBS=1). The floor is a MULTISET, so a regression at 125 goes 2>1 and reds (emerge issue #99 was refuted and the semantics pinned by test); the one undetectable shape is a single change that kills this occurrence AND introduces an identical-text untested line — documented as a KNOWN LIMIT of drift-tolerant keying.

# platform-unreachable on this Windows host: inside `#[cfg(unix)] fn open_private_new` (the atomic mode-0600 keyfile open). Killable only on a unix runner, where dropping write(true) fails the first key write.
# platform-unreachable on this Windows host: same #[cfg(unix)] open_private_new — create_new(true→false) would follow a pre-planted symlink instead of refusing, observable only on unix.
# platform-unreachable here, deliberately unobservable everywhere: `#[cfg(unix)] fn sync_parent_dir` is the same best-effort dir-fsync pattern — the result is discarded by design.
